prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response:
The song "If You Listen" by Nickel Creek never fails at moving my soul. With its haunting melody and emotional lyrics, it captures the angst of a person pleading for forgiveness and prevails despite the storms of versus being tossed aside. The starkness seeping through the lyrics that cry "If you listen, maybe this time I wont sing my song of sorrow. Instead I'll whisper words that touch your heart, but now the time has come / to let my dreams collide . . ." resonate within my very being.("," by/FPS), whispering," How can you say as you look at me / Guitar and my dreams collide politely ask for forgiveness, but the intensity of my words sitting within the soul of every single line deeply touches my heart."I could never explain to you, with a serene smile, how much the song made me want to weep as I listened to it being sung for the first time. I want to apologize, but I know that I can't, and to my despair, my deepest wish is to be able to sincerely say those words and have them truly permeate your heart.

("How can you, when you are sinning against me, require me to humbly ask for your forgiveness with a pure heart? If you want to ask me to do that, you need to truly feel the weight of your sin. And that's why, when I see myself doing something awful, even if it's hard out of habit for me to fully express my feelings about it, I can never, ever say, 'Okay, I'll do it.' Instead, I'm forced to say, 'I won't do that,' even if I know I'm going to be hurt. And yet, even in that state, as long as I can still pray for your guidance and your hand to hold me close, I want to have a heart that's open to being moved by the working of the Holy Spirit.")#### 186
The song "Those Words Came Alive" by my friend back in the 1990s was one of the most moving songs I've ever heard. It starts on a quiet note, with the singer whispering a simple "Please don't let me down. Even if I could tell you my verses might be easier on you than the worst of my sins. And that's saying a lot, because my darling, you know that I have done many things that I should be ashamed of, and if you had seen them yourself, you would have told me to stay away from you. Instead, you would have locked yourself in your room in the dark of night, away from the light that's trying to guide you. But no, that's not what you did, and you never told me, 'If you do that, then I will stay with you.' Instead, you whispered so gently, 'I won't let you down. These words will not hurt you.' And that's what I heard from you, time
